message:
test(assign/capability): extend TestDefaultCapabilities to cover all seven agent types

Follow-on to the preceding `feat(assign/effectiveness): rank
against all supported agent types` commit. That commit extended
`EffectivenessIntegrator.RankAgentsForTask` to walk all seven
`tmux.AgentType` values (Claude, Codex, Gemini, Cursor,
Windsurf, Aider, Ollama) so the effectiveness ranker would
score every supported provider. This commit catches the
companion `TestDefaultCapabilities` test up to the same list:

The test previously only verified that `DefaultCapabilities`
had entries for the original three agent types:

    agents := []tmux.AgentType{tmux.AgentClaude, tmux.AgentCodex, tmux.AgentGemini}

A regression that removed the `Cursor`, `Windsurf`, `Aider`, or
`Ollama` entries from `DefaultCapabilities` would not have been
caught because those agents weren't iterated in the test loop.
Extend the slice to include all seven so the missing-agent
guard covers the full set:

    agents := []tmux.AgentType{
        tmux.AgentClaude, tmux.AgentCodex, tmux.AgentGemini,
        tmux.AgentCursor, tmux.AgentWindsurf, tmux.AgentAider, tmux.AgentOllama,
    }

The per-agent assertion body is unchanged — for each agent
the test still checks `DefaultCapabilities[agent]` is present
and non-empty via the same ok-check.
